prompt:
Theorem title: Pinsker's inequality via reduction to the Bernoulli case

Ambient theorem statement (context only; do not formalize this whole statement unless it exactly matches the target node):
Pinsker's inequality via reduction to the Bernoulli case.

Let \((X,\mathcal F)\) be a measurable space, and let \(P\) and \(Q\) be probability measures on \((X,\mathcal F)\). Assume \(P\ll Q\), so that the Kullback–Leibler divergence
\[
D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)
:=
\int_X \log\!\left(\frac{dP}{dQ}\right)\,dP
\]
is well defined in \([0,\infty]\).

Then
\[
D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\,\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}^2,
\]
where
\[
\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}:=\sup_{A\in\mathcal F}|P(A)-Q(A)|.
\]

Equivalently, if \(\delta:=\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}\), then
\[
D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\delta^2.
\]

The key local scalar step is the Bernoulli inequality
\[
p\log\!\left(\frac{p}{q}\right)+(1-p)\log\!\left(\frac{1-p}{1-q}\right)\ge 2(p-q)^2,
\qquad 0<p,q<1,
\]
which appears after collapsing the general measures to a two-point partition.

Primary formalization target: the target node's informal statement and informal proof text below. Do not try to prove the ambient theorem statement itself unless it is identical to the target node.

Target theorem name: bernoulli_ineq_aristotle

Scratch file to rewrite: FormalIslands/Generated/bernoulli_ineq_worker_20260406-071621_3c2f3e96.lean

Target node:

- id: bernoulli_ineq
- title: Bernoulli/binary KL scalar inequality
- informal statement:
For all $p,q\in(0,1)$, $p\log\!(p/q)+(1-p)\log\!((1-p)/(1-q))\ge 2(p-q)^2$.
- informal proof text:
Define $F_q(p):=p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))-2(p-q)^2$ for $p\in(0,1)$ with $q\in(0,1)$ fixed. By direct substitution $F_q(q)=0$. By direct computation $F_q'(q)=0$. By the convexity lemma for $F_q$, $F_q$ is convex on $(0,1)$, so $p=q$ is a global minimizer. Therefore $F_q(p)\ge F_q(q)=0$ for all $p\in(0,1)$.
- formalization priority: 3
- formalization rationale:
Promoted after verified child 'bernoulli_ineq_refined_local_claim' certified a local core; attempting broader parent coverage.

  - [setup] Define $F_q(p):=p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))-2(p-q)^2$ for $p\in(0,1)$ with $q\in(0,1)$ fixed.
  - [identity_or_estimate] By direct substitution $F_q(q)=0$.

Context-only sibling ingredients in the same proof neighborhood:

- id: root
  title: Pinsker's inequality
  informal statement: Let $P,Q$ be probability measures on $(X,\mathcal{F})$ with $P\ll Q$. Then $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge 2\,\|P-Q\|_{\mathrm{TV}}^2$.
- id: dpi
  title: Data-processing inequality for KL divergence
  informal statement: Let $P,Q$ be probability measures on $(X,\mathcal{F})$ with $P\ll Q$, and let $T:(X,\mathcal{F})\to(Y,\mathcal{G})$ be any measurable map. Then $D_{\mathrm{KL}}(P\|Q)\ge D_{\mathrm{KL}}(T_\#P\|T_\#Q)$.
- id: fq_convex
  title: Convexity of $F_q$ via second-derivative bound
  informal statement: For fixed $q\in(0,1)$, define $F_q(p):=p\log(p/q)+(1-p)\log((1-p)/(1-q))-2(p-q)^2$ on $p\in(0,1)$. Then $F_q''(p)=\tfrac{1}{p(1-p)}-4\ge 0$, so $F_q$ is convex on $(0,1)$.
